title: Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis
chief_complaint: I've had sinus pain, nosebleeds, and coughing up blood for 2 months. My urine looks dark like cola. I have a rash on my legs and my joints ache. I've lost 15 pounds. Antibiotics haven't helped.

physical_exam.ENT: Nasal crusting, saddle-nose deformity beginning. Nasal septum perforation visible. Sinus tenderness over maxillary sinuses.
physical_exam.Skin: Palpable purpura on lower extremities. Necrotic ulcers on fingertips.
physical_exam.Joints: Synovitis in both wrists and knees.
physical_exam.Eyes: Episcleritis right eye (redness without pain or vision change).
physical_exam.Neurologic: Right foot drop (mononeuritis multiplex from vasculitis of vasa nervorum).

hidden_data.cxr: Multiple bilateral cavitary nodules in upper and lower lobes. Some with air-fluid levels. No pleural effusion.
hidden_data.ct_chest: Multiple cavitary pulmonary nodules, some with surrounding ground-glass hemorrhage. Bronchial wall thickening.
hidden_data.cmp: Creatinine 2.8 (rapidly rising - RPGN), BUN 52. Urine PCR 2.5g/day.
hidden_data.anca: c-ANCA positive (1:640). Anti-PR3 antibody positive (confirmatory). p-ANCA/MPO negative.
hidden_data.renal_biopsy: Pauci-immune crescentic glomerulonephritis. >50% crescents on biopsy. Fibrinoid necrosis. No immune complex deposition (pauci-immune).
hidden_data.sinus_ct: Pansinusitis with bony erosion. Nasal septum perforation.

correct_diagnosis: Granulomatosis with Polyangiitis (GPA - formerly Wegener's) - Generalized with Pulmonary-Renal Syndrome, Mononeuritis Multiplex, and Purpura
key_learning_points: GPA = ANCA-associated necrotizing granulomatous vasculitis of small and medium vessels. Classic triad: Upper respiratory (sinusitis, saddle nose, septal perforation) + Lower respiratory (cavitary lung nodules, hemoptysis) + Kidney (RPGN)
c-ANCA/PR3 positive in 90% of GPA (p-ANCA/MPO more common in microscopic polyangiitis). Renal biopsy: Pauci-immune crescentic GN (no immune deposits on immunofluorescence)
Pulmonary-renal syndrome = diffuse alveolar hemorrhage + rapidly progressive GN. Can also be caused by Goodpasture syndrome (anti-GBM disease - look for linear IgG deposits on IF) and SLE

Treatment: Induction: High-dose steroids + Rituximab (anti-CD20) OR Cyclophosphamide. Plasma exchange if DAH or Cr >5.7. Maintenance: Rituximab or Azathioprine + low-dose steroids. Treat PJP prophylaxis with Bactrim

Without treatment: 90% mortality in 2 years (usually from renal failure). With treatment: 80% remission rate. Relapse common (50% within 5 years). Monitor ANCA titers (not perfect but rising titer may precede relapse)
